flashcards.back: A malicious technique that tricks a user into clicking on a hidden interactive element, causing them to perform an unintended action on a different website.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the X-Frame-Options header?
flashcards.back: To instruct the browser on whether a page can be rendered within a <frame>, <iframe>, <embed>, or <object> to avoid clickjacking.

flashcards.front: What does the 'DENY' directive for X-Frame-Options do?
flashcards.back: It prevents the page from being displayed in any frame, regardless of the originating site.
The frame-ancestors directive within the Content-Security-Policy header.

flashcards.front: What is a key weakness of client-side JavaScript framebusting?
flashcards.back: It can be bypassed by an attacker using the iframe sandbox attribute to disable JavaScript execution.
